Echinacea rosea, or Rose coneflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing the severity and duration of colds and flu, as well as boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea rosea include alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids, among others. Echinacea rosea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its potential benefits.
